name: Flandre Scarlet
_design_notes: ECL mapping: wiki/flandre-th06-reconstruction.md — dump ecldata7_bullets.txt; summarize with tools/th06-ecl-dump (chunk ids like ecldata7_bullets.txt#sub:Sub33).
TH06 Extra Stage boss (ecldata7 / Stage 7). Spell order: Cranberry Trap → Laevatein → Four of a Kind → Kagome Kagome → Maze of Love → Forbidden Barrage Starbow Break.
Taboo「フォーオブアカインド」: TH06 uses clone familiars. This build implements logical phase emitters (no sprite) so clone-origin fire can be reproduced from the correct off-screen positions.
Patchouli midboss (Extra) is not a separate arena boss here; fairy stage follows ecldata7 Timeline0 through Sub8 with a gap where Sub16 would run.
Nonspells map to ECL drivers Sub32 / Sub35 / Sub38 / Sub47 / Sub49 (ins_70 fans + ins_50 radial bursts), not Remilia Sub20-style laser opens.
Music: U.N. Owen was her? — track id u_n_owen_was_her. Patterns use existing PatternStep only.
_validation_matrix: EASY/NORMAL: Cranberry reads as top rain + slow large scarlet walls + occasional bounces; Laevatein readable laser sweep + chunky volleys; Four of a Kind clearly three off-center pressure corners (not three identical timers); Kagome edges fire at different cadences; Maze emphasizes hearts + ring squeeze distinct from Cranberry rain; Starbow multicolor spokes + rotating lasers without unreadable overlap.

HARD/LUNATIC: Same spell identities with higher density — clone emitters stay phase-offset (staggered cooldowns / arms) so Lunatic is not 'same JSON, more HP'; nonspells scale arms/cooldown via existing difficulty sampling without converging to one generic fan.
Emitters: positions follow TH-scaled coords in phase _note; attack stacks intentionally differ per emitter so ins_95 clone subs are not one copied block.
Regression: boss phases remain network-safe (no new packet shapes); emitter bullets use same pool rules as boss-origin patterns.

introDialog.speaker: BOSS
introDialog.text: I was sooo bored down here… so I found something fun! If you break, it means I found the right spot, okay?

reimu.speaker: BOSS
reimu.text: You've got lots of little shiny threads… which one do I touch to make you go *pop*?

reimu.speaker: PLAYER
reimu.text: I'm not here to play your games.

reimu.speaker: BOSS
reimu.text: But breaking things is a game! If I touch the right place, something interesting always happens!

marisa.speaker: BOSS
marisa.text: Wow… you sparkle a lot! If I poke you, will you scatter everywhere?

marisa.speaker: PLAYER
marisa.text: Heh, careful, they bite back, ze.

marisa.speaker: BOSS
marisa.text: Bite? Heehee… then I just have to find where the bite stops!

sakuya.speaker: BOSS
sakuya.text: Sakuya… you feel like a line stretched too tight. If I pull it just a little..

sakuya.speaker: PLAYER
sakuya.text: If you attempt that, I will stop you immediately.

sakuya.speaker: BOSS
sakuya.text: Heehee… then try. I want to see which of us breaks first.

sanae.speaker: BOSS
sanae.text: Hmm… you feel weird. Like you're not built the same. Where's your weak spot?

sanae.speaker: PLAYER
sanae.text: Whatever you're thinking, it won't work on me.

sanae.speaker: BOSS
sanae.text: Heehee… that just means I have to look extra carefully!

cirno.speaker: BOSS
cirno.text: Oh! You look easy to understand! I bet I can figure you out quickly!

cirno.speaker: BOSS
cirno.text: Strong? That's fine! Strong things are the most fun when they suddenly stop working!

victoryDialog: Eh? It stopped already? I didn't even find the fun part yet…
defeatDialog: Ah… I see it! So that's where everything ends… how pretty…

victoryDialogByCharacter.reimu: Huh… I couldn't find anything interesting inside at all…
victoryDialogByCharacter.marisa: Aww… it all just faded away. That's boring…
victoryDialogByCharacter.sakuya: You kept slipping away… I couldn't catch the ending.
victoryDialogByCharacter.sanae: You were strange… but you still ended the same way.
victoryDialogByCharacter.cirno: Huh? You just stopped? That was quick!

defeatDialogByCharacter.reimu: Oh! There it is! That's the spot!
defeatDialogByCharacter.marisa: Whoa… it all came apart like sparks!
defeatDialogByCharacter.sakuya: Even time can't hide that point forever, it seems.
defeatDialogByCharacter.sanae: So you really did have one…
defeatDialogByCharacter.cirno: I found it! I found it first!

phases._note: ECL Sub32 nonspell before Cranberry — ins_70(2,2,64,2) wide fan + ins_50/ins_61 radial bursts (~70f loop), not Remilia-style lasers. Chunk: ecldata7_bullets.txt#sub:Sub32

ECL Taboo Cranberry Trap — red curtain + trap walls. Locate exact driver sub via tools/th06-ecl-dump index.md (ins_93 spell name → spell_driver row). Emphasis: RAIN + BOUNCE reads; keep Maze phase more ring/mentos-forward for contrast.
ECL Sub35 nonspell before Laevatein — same ins_70(2,2,64,2) family as Sub32; loop ~90f (ins_61 50) → slightly quicker radial cadence than NS1. Chunk: ecldata7_bullets.txt#sub:Sub35
Taboo Laevatein — sword/laser + large shots (LaserPool + volleys). Driver sub id from tool index (spell row). Distinct from Cranberry: more LASER_ROTATING / LASER_BEAM weight, less ceiling RAIN.
ECL Sub38 before Four of a Kind — ins_70(2,2,32,3,4.0f) tighter arc + 32-way feel; still ins_50 bursts (no boss laser web). Chunk: ecldata7_bullets.txt#sub:Sub38

ECL Sub39 Taboo Four of a Kind — clone-origin fire (Sub40/41/42) + boss volleys. Chunks: ecldata7_bullets.txt#sub:Sub39 (+ Sub40–42 via ins_95 in tool). Emitters below are intentionally not identical: TH06 uses separate subs per clone; mod staggers ring vs spread cadence and bullet families per corner.

emitters._note: Sub40 clone (-32,96) TH → (-40,137): left-corner pressure — faster offset rings, slower aimed spread.
Sub41 clone (256,-32) → (320,-46): top anchor — wider ring wall, quicker gold volleys (vertical entry read).
Sub42 clone (416,96) → (520,137): mirror-right — medium rings + rice spread for lane texture vs Sub40 STAR.

phases._note: ECL Sub47 before Maze of Love — ins_70(2,6,32,2,2.5f) six-arm layer + same ins_50/ins_61 loop as Sub35/38. Chunk: ecldata7_bullets.txt#sub:Sub47

ECL Sub44 Taboo Kagome Kagome — edge spawners (Sub45) + boss volleys. Chunks: ecldata7_bullets.txt#sub:Sub44, #sub:Sub45. Emitters: four edges use different cadence/arms so the spell is not four copy-paste RINGs.

emitters._note: Sub45 top (64,0) TH → (80,0): dense downward star ring.
Sub45 right (400,256) → (500,366): faster ORB wall, inward read.
Sub45 left (-16,160) → (-20,229): slower wide ring — space for player to hug margin.
Sub45 bottom (320,464) → (400,663): fastest spam lane — floor pressure.

phases._note: ECL Sub49 before Starbow — ins_70(2,6,32,2,3.5f) faster radial than Sub47; still fan/burst vocabulary (not Scarlet Meister-style). Chunk: ecldata7_bullets.txt#sub:Sub49
Taboo Maze of Love — heart / wall pressure. Distinct from Cranberry: higher SCARLET_MENTOS + RING_OFFSET weight, less RAIN; reads as squeeze maze not ceiling curtain.
Forbidden Barrage Starbow Break — rainbow spoke finale (all ranks).
